item.title: two months later...
item.description: Lots happened over the past 2 months. I been in the shop making, tinkering, designing and fabricating. Prepped for gradex (undergrad final year show) and exhibited for 4 days! it was so quite fun, equally taxing.

item.title: Fold typeface
item.description: Working on a folding typeface. Here's how the interface lookin!

item.title: Updates on the node tool!
item.description: Changed the theme and rewrote the implementation for the svg lines that connect the nodes together. This was a gigantic performance boost. The way I had set it up before was that for each mutation on any of the lines would re-render every single one of those lines. Not only that, it was also leaking memory because all the old svg line elements weren't correctly disposed. Anyways all that is fixed now and memory is at a steady 20-30mb.

item.title: Future of [FEED]
item.description: I wanna turn this website into an RSS Feed. Essentially anything I want to publish would be here, including stuff over at writing.a-p.space and other stuff.

item.title: Some Physarium Algorithm tests
item.description: I wanted to play around with this stuff:

item.title: Whittling Final Pieces
item.description: Submitted the first whittling assignment! This was soooooo fun!!!

item.title: Essentially...
item.description: What I seem to figure out is how are loops and functions going to work...

item.title: Dynamic connection colors
item.description: Changes color based on active link!

item.title: Node based editor for publication tool
item.description: The idea is to take the interface of are.na canvas, so positioning, zooming, draggin etc and then combine it with the engine of the publication tool, essentially giving me a node based procedural publication design tool.

item.title: Taking a woodworking class this semester
item.description: Day 1: Basic Whittling...

item.title: Moving back to Neovim :)
item.description: I've been using Emacs for the past 6-8 months and I've grown to like it quite a lot. I shifted to it when I was doing C programming and eventually wanted to do some Lisp.

item.title: UPPPPPDATES
item.description: Update Number #2 (JAN 17): since having started this post, I began a refactor and now the refactor is done... Fully rewrote the repo to make it more easier to work with and better organised and performance wise much better + added undo redo by using a reversible store object.

item.title: Making a post from Canvas
item.description: I'm updating this post from the canvas :) I think the canvas itself is almost ready and excited to share it online. There was some weird bug where when I saved the .canvas file to are.na channel and loaded it back in there was a JSON parsing error. Although it happened only for a specific block and hasn't happened since... so I dunno. Will see if this comes back later.

item.title: Updates on Are.na Canvas
item.description: Updated the UI

item.title: Nodes and connections
item.description: Working on Are.na canvas to refresh my brain on concepts on how to make a draggable + canvas based tools. Going to make the interface for the publication tool a node based interface. So this is sort of the first draft/testing space for that.

item.title: french curves
item.description: Made some sketches using french curves.

item.title: A space for writing and learning
item.description: As this year is coming to an end, I'm sowing seeds for the next year. I started writing.a-p.space, this is mostly going to be a space akin to this feed where I can document, and formulate ideas. So I can look back on these, reflect and iterate.

item.title: Making an infinite canvas for are.na
item.description: Will make use of the open file format spec'd by Obsidian. This should potentially have interoperability with obsidian.

item.title: First Post over at writing.a-p
item.description: Go check it out!

Folding Problems
item.description: I've been working on this for a month. but essentially this was the problem:

item.title: Added project pages and some view options
item.description: Added project pages, you can probably see this on the right here ->

item.title: Working on a booklet with Craig
Using instagram
item.description: I've been off instagram for almost the past 3 years. And I'm extremely happy with that decision and it has brought nothing but joy to me. However I think I need to interact with it for professional reasons.

item.title: Gave a talk today at [Creative Coding Toronto]
item.description: Presented a breakdown of the parts that constitute this publication tool I've been working on. So presented it as a library of functions written down as an interrogation of a typographic vocabulary.

item.title: Menu
item.description: The software view:

item.title: Imposition tool updates
item.description: Was working on this tool last semester, and recently need it for another project where I need to make a publication (a menu) where pages are offset vertically and horizontally.

item.title: How templater works
item.description: Overview

item.title: Templater
item.description: Playing around with static site generation. Here is a basic draft I made for a static site generator which basically compiles to a js file.
